other_observers: None on the 4th, Larry Semo on the 24th.; Larry Semo found the bird on April 4, 2004 and Eric Zorawowicz saw it on the April 7.
description: I recognized the bird as a loon as soon as I saw it (long body, thick neck, and upright posture). It was difficult to tell feather details due to the distance, but striking features that I didn't need a scope to see were the bright white parts including the entire face, entire neck except the hindneck, and sides (the bird was floating high in the water). The crown, hindneck, and back were dark gray. The light gray bill was very thin and pointed, with a slight upward angle.Behaviors: Swimming (resting on water).Call: None heard.Plumage: Basic
